steps.title: Spot the passage's two distinct movements
steps.content: The text first presents how other people regard Mr. Ely—with admiration and respect (remarkable, learned, discreet, agreeable)—then shifts to describing his specific behaviors: avoiding heated arguments, suggesting rather than asserting opinions, never visibly mocking others, never giving them grounds to mock him.

steps.title: Determine what those behaviors explain
steps.content:
These behaviors explain how Mr. Ely maintains favorable opinions—his discretion and careful conduct protect his reputation.

Choice B directly identifies this structure: presenting favorable opinion others hold, then describing the behaviors that enable him to maintain it.

  • A misreads the passage as emphasizing naivety
  • C falsely claims a decline in reputation
  • D misinterprets the behaviors as obscuring a hidden self
.

That matches choice B—B is correct.
